info.description: dsili:Illustrated Item corpus REST Services provided for Illustrated Item objects
info.title: Illustrated Item Web Services.

get.summary: Gets a indexed search result of Illustrated Item.
get.description: Gets a indexed search result of Illustrated Item.

parameters.name: $searchStr
parameters.description: A search text to find one or more items.

parameters.name: $mask
parameters.description: Mask defining what will be returned. Default Mask is:dsili:IllustratedItemMask.Default

parameters.name: $top
parameters.description: Represents the total number of items returned from the search, accepts a maximum value Instructionof 1000.

parameters.name: $skip
parameters.description: Represents the number of items to skip (to be used along with $top query parameter)

schema.example: Response will be returned with oneOf mask available in the Schema Tab.

400.description: BAD REQUEST. The request could not be understood by the server due to malformed syntax. The client SHOULD NOT repeat the request without modifications.

401.description: UNAUTHORIZED. The request requires user authentication. The response MUST include a WWW-Authenticate header field containing a challenge applicable to the requested resource. The client MAY repeat the request with a suitable Authorization header field. If the request already included Authorization credentials, then the 401 response indicates that authorization has been refused for those credentials. If the 401 response contains the same challenge as the prior response, and the user agent has already attempted authentication at least once, then the user SHOULD be presented the entity that was given in the response, since that entity might include relevant diagnostic information. HTTP access authentication is explained in "HTTP Authentication: Basic and Digest Access Authentication".

403.description: FORBIDDEN. The server understood the request, but is refusing to fulfill it. Authorization will not help and the request SHOULD NOT be repeated. If the request method was not HEAD and the server wishes to make public why the request has not been fulfilled, it SHOULD describe the reason for the refusal in the entity. If the server does not wish to make this information available to the client, the status code 404 (Not Found) can be used instead.

500.description: INTERNAL SERVER ERROR. A generic error message, given when an unexpected condition was encountered and no more specific message is suitable.

parameters.name: $mask
parameters.description: Mask defining what will be returned. Default Mask is:dsili:IllustratedItemMask.Default

parameters.name: $fields
parameters.description: We can add several fields with comma(,) separator to get in the response.

schema.example: Response will be returned with oneOf mask available in the Schema Tab.

200.description: OK. The request has succeeded. The information returned with the response is dependent on the method used in the request, for example: GET an entity corresponding to the requested resource is sent in the response; HEAD the entity-header fields corresponding to the requested resource are sent in the response without any message-body; POST an entity describing or containing the result of the action; TRACE an entity containing the request message as received by the end server.

400.description: BAD REQUEST. The request could not be understood by the server due to malformed syntax. The client SHOULD NOT repeat the request without modifications.

401.description: UNAUTHORIZED. The request requires user authentication. The response MUST include a WWW-Authenticate header field containing a challenge applicable to the requested resource. The client MAY repeat the request with a suitable Authorization header field. If the request already included Authorization credentials, then the 401 response indicates that authorization has been refused for those credentials. If the 401 response contains the same challenge as the prior response, and the user agent has already attempted authentication at least once, then the user SHOULD be presented the entity that was given in the response, since that entity might include relevant diagnostic information. HTTP access authentication is explained in "HTTP Authentication: Basic and Digest Access Authentication".

500.description: INTERNAL SERVER ERROR. A generic error message, given when an unexpected condition was encountered and no more specific message is suitable.

delete.summary: Deletes specific dsili:ItemSpecification link. Used for deleting or the scope Link or Implement Link.

200.description: OK. The request has succeeded. The information returned with the response is dependent on the method used in the request, for example: GET an entity corresponding to the requested resource is sent in the response; HEAD the entity-header fields corresponding to the requested resource are sent in the response without any message-body; POST an entity describing or containing the result of the action; TRACE an entity containing the request message as received by the end server.

403.description: FORBIDDEN. The server understood the request, but is refusing to fulfill it. Authorization will not help and the request SHOULD NOT be repeated. If the request method was not HEAD and the server wishes to make public why the request has not been fulfilled, it SHOULD describe the reason for the refusal in the entity. instancePosition.description:
Its Position of the instance in the occurrence path relative to reference item and this is not stored in database it just a temporary value to match the input and output.
'instancePosition' was additionally introduced because even if ordering Instances in specifiedOccurrence array was randomized for some reason (because of sorting or so ..) we process it in the right way.

source.description: Full path to the source, including the server hostname.

relativePath.description: Relative path from the source to get the details of the object, including the ID of the object.

source.description: Full path to the source, including the server hostname.

relativePath.description: Relative path from the source to get the details of the object, including the ID of the object.

reference.description: Reference id of the instance value
Physical id of the referenced IllustratedItemReference

reservedby.description: User who has reserved (locked) the object

expandDepth.description: The depth to which nested structures should be expanded. A value of -1 indicates maximum depth.

withPath.description: Whether to include paths in the expanded output.

dsili_bulkfetch.description: List of dsili:IllustratedItem reference physical ids. We limit 1000 physical ids to be passed per request.

dsili_instance_bulkfetch.description: List of dsili:IllustratedItemInstance instance physical ids. We limit 1000 physical ids to be passed per request.

dsili_itemspecification_bulkfetch.description: List of dsili:ItemSpecification instance physical ids. We limit 1000 physical ids to be passed per request.

referencedObjects.description: List of input objects to locate the target object. Its limited to 1000 objects per request only

filterResults.description:
This is optional. The results will be filtered based on this value.
- "source": Get the mfg process instances which has input ids as source.
- "target": Get the mfg process instances which has input ids as target.
